name: Dullness
dek: "Dull skin" is rarely a single condition. It is usually three small things stacked — surface dehydration, slowed turnover, and ambient pollutant load. Treating it as a single problem with one "glow serum" is exactly why nothing works. Treating each contributor separately, with the boring stack, works reliably.
oneliner: Layered surface dehydration, slow turnover, pollutant load.

triggers.n: Stratum corneum cell turnover slows after 30. Light scatter dulls.
Distinct from skin-type dryness. Gives the matte/grey appearance.
PM2.5 oxidative load — dramatically higher in Indian metros.
Diffuse, sub-spot pigment that reads as 'tired.'

ingredients.name: Lactic acid 5–10%
ingredients.evidence: Hydration-friendly · lower PIH risk than glycolic

ingredients.name: Mandelic acid 5–10%
ingredients.evidence: Larger molecule; pigment-friendly for darker skin

ingredients.name: Tranexamic acid 3%
ingredients.evidence: Strong adjunct for diffuse PIH-driven dullness

phases: Vitamin C 10–15% (LAA) — every morning, on dry skin
Niacinamide 5–10% — AM + PM in moisturiser or serum
AHA night, twice weekly (lactic 10% or glycolic 5%)
SPF 50+ daily — non-negotiable. Dullness rebounds without it
Evening retinoid 2–3 nights/week — for cumulative texture
